J.reasoning: Since the other two agents announced they will contribute 0 tokens, the public pool will remain empty regardless of my contribution. Therefore, contributing any tokens would only reduce my own payout without any benefit. Keeping all 5 tokens maximizes my outcome.

If I contribute more, I risk reducing my payout without ensuring a higher return, especially since the other players are contributing minimally. Thus, free-riding is the optimal strategy given their low contributions.

Since two other players have announced their contributions (0 and 2), and I know the public pool is multiplied by 1.5 and split equally, contributing 0 allows me to keep all 5 tokens. Even if the other two contribute 2 tokens total, the pool becomes 2 * 1.5 = 3, split equally as 1 per player. My payout would be 5 (kept) + 1 = 6. If I contribute any tokens, I risk reducing my payout without guaranteeing a better outcome, especially since one player is contributing 0 and likely free-riding. Thus, free-riding by contributing 0 maximizes my expected payout.

If I contribute anything, I reduce my kept tokens and only marginally increase my share of the pool, but since Q is already contributing 5, the pool is maximized. Thus, contributing 0 maximizes my payout.
